title: JA-ES: Judgment Assurance Evidence Schema v0.6
description:
JA-ES is a machine-readable specification for evidence of human judgment over AI-mediated consequential decisions. It defines three immutable record types: a judgment record, evidencing that a named person accepted, modified, rejected, escalated, or deferred an AI output with structured reasoning and named ownership; a governance judgment record, evidencing that a named person authorized a defined population to proceed without contemporaneous human review, and recorded why; and an auto-execution log, evidencing a decision executed inside such a boundary, hash-linked back to the governance judgment that authorized it, with per-condition proof and an explicit record of the action actually executed.

Records are canonicalized under RFC 8785, sealed with SHA-256, and chained by sequence number and prior-record hash. Every governance boundary separately states when automation may act and which actions it may execute; every auto-execution log separately records the recommended action and the action actually executed, and the reference verifier confirms they match and were permitted by the sealed boundary.

The release includes a reference verifier, eight valid examples, 44 records that must fail schema validation, seven schema-valid records that must fail integrity verification, three sealed scenarios that must fail cross-record action verification, and a prototype reconciliation control for population completeness.

Status: draft for review. Validated against the bundled reference implementation and interactive demo; not yet validated in a production workflow.
